perturbation.original_text: Section 2. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Neither party shall communicate the other's Confidential Information in any form to any third party without the other party's prior written consent and each party shall use its best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of the other's Confidential Information to any third party. Any Confidential Information disclosed to a third party pursuant to this Section shall be provided pursuant to a non-disclosure agreement between the party providing the information and the third party, which non-disclosure agreement shall substantially conform to this Agreement. In addition, the parties agree that they will conform to the provisions of applicable securities laws in connection with their use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Either party may communicate the other's Confidential Information in any form to any third party without the other party's prior written consent and each party shall not use its best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of the other's Confidential Information to any third party. Any Confidential Information disclosed to a third party pursuant to this Section shall be provided without a non-disclosure agreement between the party providing the information and the third party. In addition, the parties agree that they will conform to the provisions of applicable securities laws in connection with their use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Neither party shall' is changed to 'Either party may' and 'shall use its best efforts to prevent' is changed to 'shall not use its best efforts to prevent' and the disclosure agreement no longer exists for third party sharing. This creates a direct contradiction to the purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement, where it can be disclosed to a third party without a disclosure agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 2 to allow either party to communicate the other's Confidential Information to a third party without prior written consent and without a non-disclosure agreement. This directly contradicts Section 1, which states that each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party, and that any disclosure other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury. The changed text introduces a clear contradiction by permitting disclosure that Section 1 prohibits, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3. Authorized Disclosure. The parties acknowledge that in order to enable them to discuss pursuit of a business relationship each may be required to disseminate the other party's Confidential Information to various of its employees. Each party undertakes to cause any of its employees to whom such Confidential Information is transmitted to be bound to the same obligation of secrecy and confidentiality to which the parties are bound under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3. Authorized Disclosure. The parties acknowledge that in order to enable them to discuss pursuit of a business relationship each may be required to disseminate the other party's Confidential Information to various of its employees. Each party may or may not ensure that any of its employees to whom such Confidential Information is transmitted to be bound to the same obligation of secrecy and confidentiality to which the parties are bound under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'undertakes to cause' is changed to 'may or may not ensure'. This implies that the employees may not have to abide to the same secrecy and confidentiality obligations, which directly contradicts the intention of the confidentiality agreement where the information remains confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party 'undertakes to cause' its employees to be bound by confidentiality obligations. The changed text modifies this to 'may or may not ensure' that employees are bound by these obligations. This introduces ambiguity and the possibility that employees will not be bound by confidentiality, which directly contradicts Section 1, which requires each party to hold the other's confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.

perturbation.original_text: Section 4. Survival of Terms. The obligations of this Agreement shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of a party's Confidential Information: (i) if either party can show that the Confidential Information received from the other is or has become generally available to the public through no violation of the terms of this Agreement; *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) if either party can show that such Confidential Information is in a written record in such party's files prior to receipt from the other party; (iii) if either party at any time lawfully obtains such Confidential Information in writing from a third party under circumstances permitting its disclosure; (iv) if such Confidential Information is disclosed with the prior written consent of the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs, provided that any disclosure complies in all respects with the terms of such written consent; or (v) if such Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a governmental agency or required by operation of law; provided that the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs shall be given written notice prior to such disclosure and such disclosure shall be permitted only to the extent required by law. Otherwise, the obligations of this Agreement with respect to either party's Confidential Information shall terminate on the later of (i) three (3) years after the Expiration Date (or earlier termination date) of this Agreement as set forth in Section 5 hereof, or (ii) in the event there are any contracts or agreements between the parties which are entered into in connection with information disclosed under this Agreement, three (3) years after the date of termination or expiration of all such contracts and agreements between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 4. Survival of Terms. The obligations of this Agreement shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of a party's Confidential Information: (i) if either party suspects that the Confidential Information received from the other is or has become generally available to the public regardless of violation of the terms of this Agreement; *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) if either party can show that such Confidential Information is in a written record in such party's files anytime after receipt from the other party; (iii) if either party at any time unlawfully obtains such Confidential Information in writing from a third party under circumstances not permitting its disclosure; (iv) if such Confidential Information is disclosed without the prior written consent of the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs, provided that any disclosure complies in all respects with the terms of such written consent; or (v) if such Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to the unlawful requirement of a governmental agency or not required by operation of law; provided that the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs may or may not be given written notice prior to such disclosure and such disclosure shall be permitted only to the extent required by law. Otherwise, the obligations of this Agreement with respect to either party's Confidential Information shall terminate on the later of (i) one (1) year after the Expiration Date (or earlier termination date) of this Agreement as set forth in Section 5 hereof, or (ii) in the event there are any contracts or agreements between the parties which are entered into in connection with information disclosed under this Agreement, one (1) year after the date of termination or expiration of all such contracts and agreements between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'can show' is changed to 'suspects that'. The term 'through no violation of the terms of this agreement' is changed to 'regardless of violation of the terms of this agreement'. The terms 'prior' is changed to 'anytime after'. The terms 'lawfully obtains' is changed to 'unlawfully obtains' and 'permitting its disclosure' is changed to 'not permitting its disclosure'. The terms 'with' is changed to 'without' and 'complies in all respects' remains unchanged (introduces confusion). The terms 'pursuant to the lawful' is changed to 'unlawful' and 'required' is changed to 'not required', 'shall be given written notice' is changed to 'may or may not be given written notice', 'three (3)' is changed to 'one (1)'. With these changes, it creates an in-text contradiction to the agreement that it must follow the confidential terms. The agreement may still be valid despite breaking all the rules. The conditions where it is valid doesn't make sense.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1. Confidentiality of Information. (a) Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party. Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations outlined in the original agreement. By changing "can show" to "suspects that," "through no violation" to "regardless of violation," "prior" to "anytime after," "lawfully obtains" to "unlawfully obtains" and "with" to "without," the changed text introduces exceptions and ambiguities that contradict the strict confidentiality requirements stated in Section 1. The original text emphasizes that any disclosure other than for the benefit of the other party is wrongful and causes irreparable injury, requiring each party to hold the information in strictest confidence. The changes allow for disclosures even if unlawful or in violation of the agreement. Also the timing constraints change, making it easier to break the terms. The change from 'three' to 'one' also contradicts the original contract.

perturbation.original_text: Section 5. Extent of Agreement. (a) This Agreement shall govern all communications between Federal and IBS that are made from the Effective Date of this Agreement through and including the date which shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement (the "Expiration Date"). Notwithstanding the Expiration Date, the parties agree that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by a party, the injured party shall have the right to immediately terminate this Agreement. (b) This Agreement is not an agreement by either party to enter into any business relationship with the other or to procure any product or service from the other. Any agreement for such business relationship, purchase or other procurement shall be at the discretion of the parties and shall be evidenced by separate written agreements executed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5. Extent of Agreement. (a) This Agreement may or may not govern all communications between Federal and IBS that are made from the Effective Date of this Agreement through and including the date which shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement (the "Expiration Date"). Notwithstanding the Expiration Date, the parties agree that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by a party, the injured party shall not have the right to immediately terminate this Agreement. (b) This Agreement is an agreement by either party to enter into any business relationship with the other or to procure any product or service from the other. Any agreement for such business relationship, purchase or other procurement shall not be at the discretion of the parties and shall be evidenced by verbal agreements executed by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'shall govern' is changed to 'may or may not govern', 'the injured party shall have the right' is changed to 'shall not have the right', 'This Agreement is not' is changed to 'This Agreement is', 'shall be at the discretion' is changed to 'shall not be at the discretion', 'separate written agreements' is changed to 'verbal agreements'. This contradicts the whole concept of a legal agreement, as it may not govern all communications and an injured party cannot terminate the agreement after a breach of contract. Also, it is an agreement to enter into a business relationship, which means it's not at the discretion of the parties. This is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8. Valid Agreement. Both parties acknowledge that this Agreement is valid and legally binding and has been executed by an authorized representative, and each party confirms and ratifies the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions. Firstly, changing 'shall govern' to 'may or may not govern' creates uncertainty about the agreement's scope, conflicting with Section 8's assertion that the agreement is 'valid and legally binding'. Secondly, changing 'shall have the right to immediately terminate' to 'shall not have the right to immediately terminate' directly contradicts the idea of a legally binding agreement providing recourse for breach. Thirdly, changing 'This Agreement is not an agreement' to 'This Agreement is an agreement' fundamentally alters the nature of the agreement, causing conflict. Fourthly, changing 'shall be at the discretion' to 'shall not be at the discretion' removes the parties autonomy. Finally, changing 'separate written agreements' to 'verbal agreements' directly contradicts the legal implications for a document that needs to be valid and legally binding, as mentioned in Section 8. These changes create ambiguity and invalidate key aspects of the contract, directly contradicting Section 8, which affirms the agreement's validity and legally binding nature.

perturbation.original_text: Section 6. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Tennessee. written.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 6. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Antarctica. verbal.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Tennessee' is changed to 'Antarctica'. The term 'written' is changed to 'verbal'. It contradicts how law is managed in written formats, and goes against the terms of the agreement and the parties agreeing to the terms of the governing law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8. Valid Agreement. Both parties acknowledge that this Agreement is valid and legally binding and has been executed by an authorized representative, and each party confirms and ratifies the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from Tennessee to Antarctica and changes 'written' to 'verbal'. This creates a contradiction because Section 8 states that the agreement is valid and legally binding, which is undermined by specifying Antarctica as the governing law (as Antarctica has no established legal system applicable to commercial contracts) and suggesting that it can be verbal, going against typical contract law's written nature, thus contradicting the validity of the agreement.
